remaining_tasks.name: Connect Vercel, Render, and UptimeRobot dashboards
remaining_tasks.progress: Vercel: live and connected. Render: blocked on billing verification, card being added, hit rate-limit. UptimeRobot: not started (depends on Render being live).

blockers.description: Render now requires a verified credit card on file to deploy free-tier Web Services (confirmed real Render policy as of 2026, not a config bug — render.yaml already correctly sets plan: free, no charge expected while on Free plan)
blockers.workaround: User is adding a card to their Render account now

blockers.description: Render rate-limited the account after "too many attempts" during card verification / deploy retries
blockers.workaround: Wait before retrying card verification or Blueprint connect

human_actions_pending.action: Finish adding verified card to Render account, then retry Blueprint connect (New -> Blueprint -> connect repo, reads committed render.yaml) once the rate-limit clears
human_actions_pending.context: Render Web Services on the Free plan require card verification for anti-abuse; render.yaml (plan: free) is already correct and pushed to origin/main

human_actions_pending.action: Fill Render's 4 sync:false env vars (SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET) with the same values as local backend/.env
human_actions_pending.context: D-07: one shared Supabase project for local + prod

human_actions_pending.action: Set up UptimeRobot monitor targeting https://macost-api.onrender.com/health, interval under 15 minutes
human_actions_pending.context: D-08 keep-alive for Render free-tier cold start; only reachable once Render deploy is live

human_actions_pending.action: Report back the live Vercel URL and confirm Render /health is live
human_actions_pending.context: Needed to run the plan's final verification (health check, CORS check) and write 01.1-03-SUMMARY.md

decisions.decision: Stick with Render (add verified card) instead of migrating to Cloudflare Workers Python
decisions.rationale: Cloudflare Workers Python Free plan needs no card, but it runs on Pyodide/WASM (not a standard uvicorn ASGI process) — would require rewriting render.yaml into a wrangler config and verifying supabase-py / pydantic-core (Rust-based) compatibility in that runtime. Too risky with MVP due 2026-07-09/10 and expo 2026-07-14 (~1 week out at time of decision). Render path keeps all of Wave 1-2's committed work (render.yaml, CORS, docker-compose) valid with zero migration.

decisions.decision: Pushed all Wave 1 + Wave 2 Task 1 commits to origin/main before dashboard setup
decisions.rationale: Vercel's import and Render's Blueprint both read from the GitHub remote, not the local machine — render.yaml specifically must exist on origin for Render's Blueprint auto-detect to work

next_action: Once Day confirms Render's card verification succeeded and the Blueprint deploy is live: (1) verify GET https://macost-api.onrender.com/health returns {"status":"ok"}, (2) confirm the live Vercel origin can call the Render API without a CORS error, (3) walk Day through the UptimeRobot monitor setup, (4) spawn a continuation gsd-executor agent for plan 01.1-03 Task 2 to write 01.1-03-SUMMARY.md and complete the plan, (5) run gsd-verifier (verify_phase_goal step) for phase 01.1 goal achievement.
context_notes: Wave 1 (01.1-01 docker-compose stack, 01.1-02 CORS+render.yaml) executed in parallel worktrees, merged clean, pushed. Wave 2 (01.1-03) Task 1 (CLAUDE.md docs) done and merged via fast-forward + pushed. Task 2 is the plan's only checkpoint:human-action gate. Docker Desktop was unreachable in the 01.1-01 executor's sandbox, so full `docker compose up --build` smoke test is still outstanding — worth a manual check once things are unblocked, though it's not what's currently blocking phase completion.
